title: Crate Value Representations Production Contract

included: Array+Compressed integer and float array decoding via the compression primitives
Half-precision (Imath 1+5+10) decoding for scalars and vectors
deferred: Splines, Relocates, PathExpression decoding (UnsupportedUsdcFeature when encountered)

layout.payload: bytes 0 through 5 (6 bytes); interpretation depends on type and flags
layout.type: byte 6, unsigned 8-bit integer indexing the type table (§16.3.10.1)

flags.array_bit: bit 63 of the 8-byte LE word (byte 7, mask 0x80) — value is an array
flags.inlined_bit: bit 62 of the 8-byte LE word (byte 7, mask 0x40) — value is inlined in payload
flags.compressed_bit: bit 61 of the 8-byte LE word (byte 7, mask 0x20) — array payload is compressed
flags.errata_note: AOUSD §16.3.9 prose ('last bit / second-last bit / third-last bit') is positionally ambiguous; the high-bit reading is normative for this repository because real .usdc files (and Pixar _ValueRep _IsArrayBit = 1ull<<63 etc.) use bits 63/62/61. A reader must not also accept the low-bit layout (0x01/0x02/0x04) as a fallback.

valid: no flags: offset-backed scalar at payload (the offset is the 6-byte payload interpreted as uint64)
inlined only: scalar value directly in first 4 payload bytes
array only: offset-backed uncompressed array (8B count + element bytes at offset)
array + compressed: offset-backed compressed array (compressed integer/float array as defined in crate-compression)

laws: the 8-byte word is read once as a single little-endian uint64; multiple per-byte reads to extract type/flags are a contract violation per the performance contract
dispatch is driven by the 1-byte type code and 1-byte flags only; field-name substring matches and section-payload text scans are contract violations
the combinations 'inlined + array', 'inlined + compressed', and 'inlined + array + compressed' must produce a clear diagnostic (MalformedUsdcValueRepresentation)

inlined_values.payload_window: only the first 4 bytes of the 6-byte payload may encode data
inlined_values.zero_payload_meaning: implicit empty/zero-valued instance of the type
optionally_inlined_types: double (if the value is identical when cast to float)
int64 (if the value is in range of int32)
uint64 (if the value is in range of uint32)
matrixXd (if diagonal with int8_t-exact diagonal elements)

dimensional_payload_rules.single_dimension: payload is the inlined value directly cast to the type
dimensional_payload_rules.two_dimensions: payload holds contiguous values converted to the element type
dimensional_payload_rules.three_dimensions: payload holds the top-left-to-bottom-right diagonal values (e.g. Matrix3D stores the 3 diagonal entries)
dimensional_payload_rules.half_precision_special_case: Vec2h fits in 4 bytes and inlines half-precision values directly; Vec3h and Vec4h inline only when values are representable as signed int8

laws: inlined values are not arrays and are not compressed; encountering Inlined alongside Array or Compressed is a contract violation
inlined indices (string, token, ObjectPath, asset) are uint32 Index values; resolution through the token/string/path tables is owned by usdc-spec-parser

offset_values.scalar_offset_layout: payload is a uint64 byte offset from the start of the file; the bytes at that offset are the typed scalar value
offset_values.array_offset_layout: payload is a uint64 byte offset from the start of the file; at that offset, an 8B unsigned 64-bit element count precedes the array data
offset_values.empty_array_sentinel: if the payload is zero, the array is considered empty (§16.3.9.3)
offset_values.small_array_compression_rule: arrays of 16 bytes or fewer should not be compressed; readers must accept either form

compressed_array_dispatch.integer_arrays: use crate-compression.compressed_integer_array decoding on the array's signed/unsigned integer width
compressed_array_dispatch.float_arrays: use crate-compression.compressed_float_array decoding (i-form or t-form by encoding character)
compressed_array_dispatch.non_simple_types: quaternions, matrices, and similar types may not be compressed; encountering Compressed on these types is a contract violation

laws: offset-backed scalar and array values must be returned as non-owning views into the opened payload until canonicalization runs
the array element count is uint64 (Crate >= 0.7.0); readers must not interpret it as uint32
compressed array bit set on a non-simple type (quat, matrix, etc.) is a contract violation

special_cases.exponent_31: Inf or NaN depending on mantissa
special_cases.all_zero: positive or negative zero by sign bit

laws: half reads return a target-native half representation; converting to float and back at the binary-format boundary is allowed but the canonical value reported through the value-decoder must round-trip without loss

layout.entries: alternating 4B key (Token Index) and 8B value (signed 64-bit byte offset from the current position to a value representation)

dictionary_value.empty_form: an empty dictionary is represented implicitly as inlined with zero payload
laws: dictionary keys are uint32 Token indices; reading them as strings is a contract violation
dictionary value offsets are signed 64-bit; a negative offset is valid and points backward in the payload
dictionaries may not be stored inside an array

feature_gated_types.policy: encountering these types under a version triple that does not require their support produces a clear UnsupportedUsdcFeature diagnostic; placeholder JSON output is a contract violation

laws: value type dispatch is driven by the 1-byte type code and 1-byte flags read from the 8-byte value representation word; field-name substring matches and section-payload text scans are contract violations

the Array / Inlined / Compressed bits are read from the high bits of byte 7 of the 8-byte LE word (masks 0x80 / 0x40 / 0x20). See docs/usdc-spec-errata.md#1. Accepting the low-bit layout (0x01/0x02/0x04) as a fallback is a contract violation because it hides the spec/reference divergence and lets a single decoder pass two contradictory test suites.
inlined and offset-backed scalars of the same value type must share a single decoded value shape
token, string, path, and asset Index resolution is delegated to usdc-spec-parser and consumed through table handles; this contract does not implement table parsing
compressed array decoding consumes crate-compression's primitives; this contract must not reimplement the LZ4 or codepoint decoders
value types introduced at Crate 0.10.0 or later must emit UnsupportedUsdcFeature when encountered; placeholder JSON or silent drops are contract violations
Value (indirect pointer) decoding must guard against recursion; circular pointers produce an empty value rather than an infinite loop
golden_groups: inlined scalar values for bool, int, float, token-index, ObjectPath-index
offset-backed scalar values for double, int64
inlined+array, inlined+compressed, inlined+array+compressed flag combinations produce MalformedUsdcValueRepresentation
offset-backed uncompressed array (8B count + element bytes)
offset-backed compressed integer array dispatching through crate-compression
offset-backed compressed float array (i-form and t-form)
dictionary value with token keys and offset entries (including negative offsets)
half-precision (Vec2h inlined, Vec3h with int8-representable values inlined, Vec3h offset for non-representable values)
Splines/Relocates/PathExpression produce UnsupportedUsdcFeature under minor_lt_10 fixtures
